# The Resonance of Sector 7

# A RAG EVALUATION NARRATIVE

# Character Dossiers

This baseline reference data provides precise ground-truth vectors for testing retrieval accuracy before evaluating the narrative arc.

# Main Character: Dr. Aris Thorne

AGE: 42 PROFESSION: Lead Xenobiologist at the Aethelgard Institute. DISTINCT FEATURES: A silver, mechanical prosthetic left forearm; wears a faded green lab coat. KEY ITEM: A brass-cased pocket watch that ticks backward (a gift from his late father). MOTIVATION: To prove that the crystalline structures in Sector 7 are sentient.

# Side Character 1: Commander Vaelen Vance

AGE: 51 PROFESSION: Head of Security for the Sector 7 Mining Colony. DISTINCT FEATURES: A jagged scar running from his right eye down to his jawline. KEY ITEM: A standard-issue plasma sidearm with a custom blue grip. MOTIVATION: To protect the colony workers and maintain order at all costs, viewing the crystals merely as a volatile resource. • • • • • • • • • •

# Page 1

# Side Character 2: Lyra Lin

AGE: 26 PROFESSION: Junior Data Analyst and Aris’s research assistant. DISTINCT FEATURES: Wears oversized, round spectacles that constantly slip down her nose; has a habit of chewing on her lower lip when nervous. KEY ITEM: A modified Model-9 datapad stickered with holographic nebula decals. MOTIVATION: To secure a permanent fellowship at the institute and impress Dr. Thorne. ◆ ◆ ◆

# ACT I: THE ANOMALOUS FREQUENCY

The subterranean caverns of Sector 7 hummed, but not with the familiar drone of heavy mining drills. Today, the cavern sang. Dr. Aris Thorne adjusted the collar of his faded green lab coat and leaned closer to the primary crystalline cluster. His mechanical left forearm whirred softly, the silver titanium gears micro￾adjusting as he rested his prosthetic hand against the smooth, violet surface of the mineral. "Lyra, tell me you’re catching this," Aris said, his voice a hushed whisper. "The resonance pattern has shifted from a static 440 Hz to a variable sequence." A few paces behind him, Lyra Lin frantically tapped on her modified Model-9 datapad. Her oversized spectacles slipped down her nose for the third time in a minute. She pushed them up with a thumb, her lower lip firmly caught between her teeth. "I see it, Dr. Thorne," Lyra replied, her eyes reflecting the blue glow of her screen’s holographic nebula stickers. "The spikes are occurring every 4.2 seconds. It’s... it's not random noise. It matches the Fibonacci sequence." "Sentience," Aris murmured. He reached into his pocket and pulled out his brass-cased pocket watch. He flipped the latch open, watching the hands tick backward against the brass face—a comforting, predictable anomaly in a world full of chaotic ones. "They aren't just reacting to our drills; they're trying to speak to us."

# ACT II: THE INTERRUPTION

The heavy, pressurized blast doors at the cavern's entrance hissed open, shattering the acoustic harmony. Heavy boots crunched against the loose gravel. • • • • •

# Page 2

Commander Vaelen Vance strode into the chamber, flanked by two security drones. The jagged scar on his right cheek caught the violet luminescence of the crystals, casting a harsh shadow across his face. His right hand rested heavily on the custom blue grip of his plasma sidearm. "Thorne, we have a problem," Vance barked, his voice echoing off the cavern walls. "The Board just authorized a full thermal-blasting sweep of this entire quadrant. Mining operations resume at 0600 tomorrow." Aris spun around, his silver hand clenching into a fist. "You can't do that, Vance! Look at the data. Lyra, show him the telemetry." Lyra flinched slightly at the Commander's imposing presence but held up her datapad. "Commander, the crystals are emitting an organized acoustic pattern. Thermal blasting will fracture the core matrix and destroy what could be the greatest discovery of the century." Vance didn't look at the datapad. He kept his eyes locked on Aris. "I don't care about rock music, Doctor. My job is to ensure the colony meets its quarterly quota of refined silicate. The Board says blast, we blast."

# ACT III: THE THRESHOLD

"It's not rock music, it's a conversation," Aris said, stepping between Vance and the crystalline cluster. The cavern seemed to respond to his rising anger; the violet light pulsed a deeper, angrier shade of indigo. Vance’s hand tightened on the blue grip of his weapon. "Step aside, Aris. Don't make this a security issue." Suddenly, a sharp, piercing whine erupted from Lyra’s datapad. The 4.2-second interval had broken. The crystals ceased their sequence and began emitting a single, continuous tone that vibrated through the floorboards, rattling the tools in Aris’s pockets. "Dr. Thorne!" Lyra shouted over the noise, gripping her spectacles to keep them from vibrating off her face. "The energy signature is spiking! They aren't just emitting sound anymore—they're building a localized electromagnetic pulse!" Vance drew his sidearm, the barrel glowing a faint, pre-charging blue. "I'm shutting it down." "Wait!" Aris yelled. He didn't look at Vance’s gun. Instead, he looked at his father’s pocket watch. The backward-ticking hands had stopped entirely. Aris realized the crystals weren't attacking; they were mimicking the technology around them to find a common medium. He stepped forward, bypassed Vance entirely, and pressed his

# Page 3

mechanical hand flat against the center of the vibrating core. The silver alloy acted as a conductor, bridging his own neural impulses, the institute’s tech, and the mineral grid. The piercing whine dropped instantly into a low, soothing hum. The indigo light softened to a warm amber. Vance slowly lowered his plasma sidearm, his eyes wide as the cavern stabilized. Lyra stared at her datapad, where the chaotic spikes had smoothed out into a perfect, looping waveform. "They adjusted to my prosthetic's frequency," Aris breathed, exhaling a breath he didn't know he was holding. He turned to Vance. "They aren't a resource, Commander. They're a neighbor. Tell the Board that if they blast, they aren't mining—they're committing murder." Vance looked from the amber glow of the crystals to the scar-faced reflection in his own sidearm's finish. He sighed, holstering the weapon. "You have until 0500 to get this data to the central mainframe, Thorne. After that, my hands are tied." As Vance turned and walked away, Lyra let out a massive sigh of relief, finally letting go of her lower lip. "We did it," she whispered. Aris looked down at his pocket watch. The hands had started moving again—still backward, but right on time. "No, Lyra," he said, smiling. "We just started."

# Page 4
